revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:生駒断層帯]]
'''Ikoma Fault Zone''' (生駒断層帯, いこまたんそうたい) is an active fault zone that runs north–south near the boundary between the {{ill|Osaka Plain|en|Osaka Plain|qid=Q1094103|lt=Osaka Plain|1=Osaka Plain}} and the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574917}}.
== Overview == The Ikoma Fault extends underground for approximately 38 km, from near {{ill|Hirakata|en|Hirakata|qid=Q270912|lt=Hirakata City|1=Hirakata}} in Osaka Prefecture in the north to near {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino City|1=Habikino}} in the south. The fault trace trends in a nearly north–south direction, slightly oriented NNE–SSW. It is thought to be a reverse fault, with the eastern side uplifted and the western side subsiding, and it is considered to exhibit no lateral displacement.
The fault zone is comprised of five faults – the {{ill|Ikoma Fault Zone|ja|生駒断層帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Fault Zone|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirakata Fault|ja|枚方断層|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirakata Fault|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579212|1=Hirakata Fault}}, {{ill|Katano Fault|ja|交野断層|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katano Fault|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579213|1=Katano Fault}}, {{ill|Taguchi Fault|ja|田口断層|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taguchi Fault|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579214|1=Taguchi Fault}}, and {{ill|Hotoda Fault|ja|誉田断層|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotoda Fault|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579216|1=Hotoda Fault}}. In the southern part, these faults coalesce into almost a single line, while in the north, multiple faults occur in parallel.
This fault zone is considered to have a slightly high probability (approximately 0–0.1%) of generating an earthquake within the next 30 years. Should an earthquake occur, it is estimated to be around magnitude 7 to 8. If the entire fault were to rupture, damage comparable to that of the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392882}} is predicted.
According to investigations by the Earthquake Research Committee, the recurrence interval of earthquakes along the fault is estimated at 3,000 to 6,000 years. Evidence of displacement – on the order of 2–3 m vertically – observed in the {{ill|Hotoda-yama Kofun|ja|誉田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotoda-yama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11631432}} (constructed in the 5th century) suggests that the most recent earthquake occurred sometime between 400 and 1000 years ago. Furthermore, the "Kawachi-koku Komatsudera Engi" recorded in the ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1554852}}'' indicates that, until the medieval period, the Komatsudera which once stood in what is now {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano City|1=Katano}} recorded earthquake damage in the years 723 and 985, either of which may have been caused by an earthquake along the Ikoma Fault Zone.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.jishin.go.jp/main/yosokuchizu/katsudanso/f077_ikoma.htm {{ill|Ikoma Fault Zone|ja|生駒断層帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Fault Zone|qq=}}] – Earthquake Research and Investigation Promotion Headquarters * [https://www.jishin.go.jp/main/chousa/01may2/index.htm {{ill|Evaluation of the Ikoma Fault Zone|ja|生駒断層帯の評価|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Evaluation of the Ikoma Fault Zone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – Earthquake Research Committee (June 13, 2001)
{{Japan-geo-stub}}
